{"verdict":"request_changes","summary":"The PR introduces useful local notions but several exported declarations are not at their natural level: some global hypotheses should be local, one structure carries unused endpoint parameters, and one theorem has an unused SLSC assumption.","findings":[{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/SemilocallySimplyConnected.lean","line":230,"issue":"`exists_uniquePath_neighborhood` is stated with the global typeclass `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]`, even though the file has already defined the local predicate `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt x` and the proof only needs the property at this point.","fix":"State the local theorem as `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt x → ∃ U, IsOpen U ∧ x ∈ U ∧ IsPathHomotopyTrivial U`, and derive the current typeclass version as a corollary if needed.","evidence":"The declaration reads `public theorem exists_uniquePath_neighborhood [SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X] (x : X)`, while `semilocallySimplyConnectedAt_iff_paths` immediately provides the needed local characterization."},{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/SemilocallySimplyConnected.lean","line":242,"issue":"`exists_pathConnected_slsc_neighborhood` repeats the same under-generalization: it assumes global SLSC, but its natural input is the local assumption at `x` plus local path-connectedness.","fix":"State it from `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt x` and `[LocPathConnectedSpace X]`; keep a typeclass wrapper only as a convenience theorem.","evidence":"The proof obtains `V` via `exists_uniquePath_neighborhood x` and then takes `pathComponentIn V x`; no point other than `x` requires the global class."},{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/SemilocallySimplyConnected.lean","line":295,"issue":"`TubeData (X) (x y) (n)` is over-parameterized: the fields `U`, `V`, and their openness/path/SLSC/subset properties never mention `x` or `y`. This forces identical tube data to be indexed by irrelevant endpoints.","fix":"Define `TubeData X n` and put the path endpoints only on `PathInTube`, `TubeData.toSet`, and the theorems that quantify over paths.","evidence":"The structure header is `public structure TubeData (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X] (x y : X) (n : ℕ) where`, but every field mentions only `X`, `n`, `U`, and `V`, not `x` or `y`."},{"file":"TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroupoid/BasedPath.lean","line":580,"issue":"`exists_refined_terminal_vertex` assumes `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]`, but the statement and proof only use local path-connectedness and the already bundled tube data.","fix":"Remove the `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]` hypothesis from this theorem; callers that need SLSC can keep it at the call site.","evidence":"The proof forms `W := V_last ∩ U` and returns `pathComponentIn W (endpoint α)` using `hW_open.pathComponentIn` and `isPathConnected_pathComponentIn`; it never invokes the SLSC class or `T.U_slsc`."}]}

verdict_obj.summary: The PR introduces useful local notions but several exported declarations are not at their natural level: some global hypotheses should be local, one structure carries unused endpoint parameters, and one theorem has an unused SLSC assumption.

findings.issue: `exists_uniquePath_neighborhood` is stated with the global typeclass `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]`, even though the file has already defined the local predicate `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt x` and the proof only needs the property at this point.
findings.fix: State the local theorem as `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt x → ∃ U, IsOpen U ∧ x ∈ U ∧ IsPathHomotopyTrivial U`, and derive the current typeclass version as a corollary if needed.
findings.evidence: The declaration reads `public theorem exists_uniquePath_neighborhood [SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X] (x : X)`, while `semilocallySimplyConnectedAt_iff_paths` immediately provides the needed local characterization.

findings.issue: `exists_pathConnected_slsc_neighborhood` repeats the same under-generalization: it assumes global SLSC, but its natural input is the local assumption at `x` plus local path-connectedness.
findings.fix: State it from `SemilocallySimplyConnectedAt x` and `[LocPathConnectedSpace X]`; keep a typeclass wrapper only as a convenience theorem.
findings.evidence: The proof obtains `V` via `exists_uniquePath_neighborhood x` and then takes `pathComponentIn V x`; no point other than `x` requires the global class.

findings.issue: `TubeData (X) (x y) (n)` is over-parameterized: the fields `U`, `V`, and their openness/path/SLSC/subset properties never mention `x` or `y`. This forces identical tube data to be indexed by irrelevant endpoints.
findings.fix: Define `TubeData X n` and put the path endpoints only on `PathInTube`, `TubeData.toSet`, and the theorems that quantify over paths.
findings.evidence: The structure header is `public structure TubeData (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X] (x y : X) (n : ℕ) where`, but every field mentions only `X`, `n`, `U`, and `V`, not `x` or `y`.

findings.issue: `exists_refined_terminal_vertex` assumes `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]`, but the statement and proof only use local path-connectedness and the already bundled tube data.
findings.fix: Remove the `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]` hypothesis from this theorem; callers that need SLSC can keep it at the call site.
findings.evidence: The proof forms `W := V_last ∩ U` and returns `pathComponentIn W (endpoint α)` using `hW_open.pathComponentIn` and `isPathConnected_pathComponentIn`; it never invokes the SLSC class or `T.U_slsc`.
